note: Stat values are rough fully-upgraded (Level 9) targets — armor stats shift slightly with your enchantments. The set perk is what defines each build; weapons, runic attacks, shield and relic are swappable to taste. All sets are craftable on a normal playthrough.

builds.name: Surtr's Scorched
builds.summary: The default 'best' set for most players. Huge bonus damage when you strike an enemy mid-attack, strong defense and vitality, and a Burn that melts health bars. Aggressive but forgiving — great from your first Give Me No Mercy run to GMGOW.
builds.statNote: Leans Strength + Vitality + Defense. Pump Strength gems in the armor and you hit like a truck while staying tanky enough to trade.

armor.perk: Flame Whiplash — landing an attack on an enemy that's attacking deals big bonus damage and applies Burn.
Reduces damage taken and raises Stagger resistance while you're attacking.
Builds toward a melee-damage burst as you keep landing hits.

builds.setPerk: Reward aggression: attacking into the enemy's attack is when this set hits hardest, while the wrists/waist keep you from getting staggered out of it.

loadout.name: Leviathan Axe + Blades of Chaos
loadout.why: Axe for control and ranged pokes, Blades for crowd damage and Burn synergy.

loadout.name: Winter's Bite (Axe)
loadout.why: Fast, high-damage frost combo that builds Stun for free.

loadout.name: Rampage of the Furies (Blades)
loadout.why: A flurry that shreds health and stunlocks — the best damage heavy in the game.

loadout.name: Dauntless Shield
loadout.why: Perfect-parry into a heavy counter feeds the 'attack into their attack' perk perfectly.

loadout.name: Talisman of Meign
loadout.why: Pop it for a burst of melee damage to delete elites and bosses.

picks: Frost Awaken — charge the axe with frost for bonus damage and faster Stun
Permafrost — extend freezes for guaranteed follow-up hits
Immolation — release stored heat as an AoE blast
Hyperion Slam — gap-closer that staggers groups

playstyle: Stay aggressive — the set rewards attacking into incoming attacks, so don't over-dodge.
Open with Winter's Bite to build Stun, then Rampage of the Furies when the bar is high.
Parry with Dauntless and immediately counter for the Flame Whiplash bonus.
builds.obtain: Crafted from Surtr's Hidden Trials in Muspelheim (post-game). Requires Glowing Embers and Crucible currency.
pros: Burn adds free damage over time
cons: Post-game grind to craft and upgrade

builds.name: Berserker Set
builds.summary: Maximum melee damage. Every hit can trigger a Soul Explosion and slow time (Realm Shift), letting you chunk bosses in seconds — but your defense is paper, so you live and die by your dodges and parries.
builds.statNote: All-in on Strength (and a healthy Cooldown/Luck for proc chance). Don't bother with Defense gems — you're not meant to get hit.

armor.perk: Big Strength; melee hits have a chance to trigger a Soul Explosion AoE.
Chance on hit to enter Realm Shift (slow time) for free burst windows.
Raises the proc chance and rewards sustained aggression.

builds.setPerk: Snowballing offense: the more you hit, the more Soul Explosions and Realm Shifts you trigger, which let you hit even more.

loadout.name: Blades of Chaos + Draupnir Spear
loadout.why: Fast multi-hits maximise proc chances; Spear adds ranged burst.

loadout.name: Nemean Crush (Blades)
loadout.why: Quick stun opener to set up a safe burst window.

loadout.name: Artillery of the Ancients (Spear)
loadout.why: Auto-aiming AoE that staggers and deletes single targets — ideal burst.

loadout.name: Dauntless Shield
loadout.why: Parries are your defense; a perfect parry buys a free aggression window.

loadout.name: Talisman of Meign
loadout.why: Stack its damage buff with a Realm Shift to one-shot elites.

picks: Immolation — convert heat stacks into a burst
Hyperion Slam — close distance and stagger
Honour the Fallen — quick chargeable poke
Gungnir's Onslaught — detonate planted spears for AoE

playstyle: Build Strength as high as possible and ignore Defense.
Use parries/perfect dodges as your survival tool, then unload during Realm Shift.
Keep attacking — every hit feeds the snowball of Soul Explosions.
builds.obtain: Defeat the 12 Berserker Gravestone bosses across the realms (and King Hrólf Kraki) for the pieces and materials.
pros: Realm Shift slows time for free
cons: Hard on GMGOW for newer players
Requires beating tough optional bosses to assemble

builds.name: Steinbjörn Set
builds.summary: Be an unkillable wall. The highest Defense in the game (more than the bar can even display), and taking big hits triggers lifesteal plus a stunning frost shockwave — so the harder you're hit, the more you heal and crowd-control.
builds.statNote: Stack Defense and Vitality. You want to face-tank hits to trigger Bloodthirsty Retaliation, so survivability is the whole point.

armor.perk: Bloodthirsty Retaliation — after taking significant damage, your attacks heal you for a short time.
Massive Defense; builds a protective layer while you avoid damage.
Releases a stunning frost shockwave when you take a heavy hit.

builds.setPerk: Turn damage into healing and crowd control — perfect for new players or anyone learning a hard encounter.

loadout.name: Leviathan Axe
loadout.why: Frost pairs with the set's shockwaves to keep everything frozen and stunned.

loadout.name: Hel's Touch (Axe)
loadout.why: Low damage but huge Stun — locks down whatever the shockwave didn't.

loadout.name: Ivaldi's Anvil (Axe)
loadout.why: Drops an AoE zone for sustained crowd damage while you tank.

loadout.name: Stonewall Shield
loadout.why: Absorb hits to charge it, then slam for a big stagger — pure defense.

loadout.name: Mardöll
loadout.why: Heals you and stuns the area — doubles down on the survive-and-control plan.

picks: Permafrost — longer freezes to combo with shockwave stuns
Serpent's Snare — pull-in for crowd control
Shield Strike skills — convert blocks into stagger
Charged slam follow-ups

playstyle: Don't be afraid to take hits — that's how you trigger lifesteal and the shockwave.
Freeze and stun-lock with Hel's Touch while the set keeps you topped up.
Great training-wheels set to learn a fight before swapping to Surtr's/Berserker.
builds.obtain: Crafted from materials in Vanaheim (the Jungle and Crucible) plus mini-boss drops — available mid/late game without post-game grinding.
pros: Forgiving for newcomers and hard difficulties

builds.name: Dragon Scaled Set
builds.summary: A defensive set that fights back. Perfect dodges and blocks unleash a fire burst, and you take less stagger — so you can stand your ground in the open and punish without getting knocked around.
builds.statNote: Balanced Strength/Defense with solid Runic — flexible enough to lean offensive or defensive with your gems.

armor.perk: Perfect evades/blocks release a fire burst that damages and ignites nearby enemies.
Reduces stagger taken so you keep your footing in a brawl.
Adds fire damage and rewards tight defensive timing.

builds.setPerk: Defense as offense — clean dodges and blocks become free fire AoE, so good timing is directly rewarded.

loadout.name: Blades of Chaos + Leviathan Axe
loadout.why: Blades for fire synergy, Axe for ranged control.

loadout.name: Winter's Bite (Axe)
loadout.why: Reliable damage + Stun for a balanced kit.

loadout.name: Rampage of the Furies (Blades)
loadout.why: Top-tier flurry to cash in after a perfect-evade burst.

loadout.name: Dauntless Shield
loadout.why: Perfect parries trigger the set's fire burst — aggressive defense.

loadout.name: Mardöll
loadout.why: On-demand heal + stun for sticky moments.

picks: Immolation — burst stored heat
Hyperion Slam — reposition and stagger
Frost Awaken — charged frost damage
Permafrost — extend freezes

playstyle: Hold your ground and time perfect dodges/blocks to trigger the fire burst.
Use the stagger resistance to keep attacking through chip damage.
Punish the burst window with Rampage of the Furies.
builds.obtain: Crafted from the three optional Drake/Dragon fights in Vanaheim (Dragon Tears, Claws and Scales).
pros: Stagger resistance keeps your combos going

builds.name: Lunda's Lost Armor
builds.summary: The best set you can build early and a parry-build staple all game. Successful blocks build a charge that supercharges your next attacks, so a shield-and-counter playstyle snowballs into big damage — strong even on the hardest difficulty.
builds.statNote: Strength-leaning with enough Defense to trade. Pairs best with a parry shield and aggressive blocking.

armor.perk: Blocking and parrying builds a charge that boosts your next melee attacks.
Improves block/parry windows and feeds the charge faster.
Converts the charge into extra damage on release.

builds.setPerk: Block-to-burst loop — defend well and your counters hit for a lot, making it a forgiving but high-damage parry build.

loadout.name: Leviathan Axe
loadout.why: Reliable frost damage and Stun to cash in your charged hits.

loadout.name: Winter's Bite (Axe)
loadout.why: Best all-round light runic for damage and Stun.

loadout.name: Ivaldi's Anvil (Axe)
loadout.why: AoE zone to control space while you bait parries.

loadout.name: Dauntless Shield
loadout.why: The aggressive parry shield — perfect parries build your charge instantly.

loadout.name: Talisman of Meign
loadout.why: Stack a damage burst with a full charge for huge counter hits.

picks: Frost Awaken — charged frost damage
Permafrost — extend freezes for free hits
Parry timing upgrades
Counter-attack follow-ups

playstyle: Block and perfect-parry to build the set's charge, then unleash boosted attacks.
Use Dauntless for aggressive parries; punish openings with Winter's Bite.
Cheap to craft early — a great bridge until you unlock Surtr's or the Berserker set.
builds.obtain: Crafted at Lunda's shop (the Huldra Brothers) in Svartalfheim early in the story, with common materials.
cons: Outscaled late by Surtr's/Berserker raw stats
